libraryDescription:
This library, gui.ImageLabelWindow, provides a window displaying an image label as a GUI component.
It can be used as a quick drawing window or as a window for displaying the contents of an image file.

In the standard VCSSL library GUI, GUI components are handled as int IDs, but the GUI component provided by this module is handled as theImageLabelWindow struct.

The ImageLabelWindow struct internally stores resource IDs and related data, but you normally do not need to be aware of its internals becausefunctions for creating and destroying it are provided. Direct access to its members is not recommended.
Specifically, create it with the newImageLabelWindow function and destroy it with thedeleteComponent function.

This module also provides overloads of standard GUI library functions so that the ImageLabelWindow struct can be handled almost in the sameway as in the standard library GUI.
For example, the mountComponent, setComponentSize, andsetComponentLocation functions are provided.

function0.name: newImageLabelWindow
function0.declaration: ImageLabelWindow newImageLabelWindow(int x, int y, int width, int height, string title, int graphics)
function0.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner of the window. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner of the window. 
(int type) width : The width of the window. 
(int type) height : The height of the window. 
(string type) title : The title of the window. 
(int type) graphics : The graphics resource.

function1.name: newImageLabelWindow
function1.description: Creates an image label window with its size determined automatically.

function2.name: deleteComponent
function2.parameters: (ImageLabelWindow type) windowInstance : The window to be destroyed.

function3.name: getWindow
function3.description: Returns the GUI component ID of the window.
function3.returnValue: (int type) The GUI component ID of the window.

function4.name: getImageLabel
function4.description: Returns the GUI component ID of the image label.
function4.returnValue: (int type) The GUI component ID of the image label.

function5.name: setComponentSize
function5.declaration: void setComponentSize(ImageLabelWindow &windowInstance, int width, int height)
function5.description: Sets the size of the window.
function5.parameters:
(ImageLabelWindow type) windowInstance : The target window. 
(int type) width : The width of the window. 
(int type) height : The height of the window.

function6.name: setComponentSize
function6.description: Automatically adjusts the layout of the internal components to match the current window size. Use this when the window size has been changed externally.

function7.name: getComponentSize
function7.description: Returns the size of the window.
function7.returnValue: (int[] type) The array storing the size of the window.

function8.name: fitComponentSize
function8.description: Sets the window size to fit the graphics resource exactly.

function9.name: setComponentLocation
function9.declaration: void setComponentLocation(ImageLabelWindow &windowInstance, int x, int y)
function9.description: Sets the location of the window.
function9.parameters:
(ImageLabelWindow type) windowInstance : The target window. 
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner of the window. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner of the window.

function10.name: getComponentLocation
function10.description: Returns the location of the window.
function10.returnValue: (int[] type) The array storing the location of the window.
